reviewFocus: Check whether each claim is supported by caller-sanitized evidence labels or quoted packet text.
Flag missing caveats when evidence is summarized, partial, stale, or caller-owned.
Flag any claim that implies approval, release readiness, remediation ownership, or external action without explicit supporting evidence.
Check that the result can be represented as the structured review-result contract with generic finding keys when useful.
Keep target selection, severity budgets, approval, routing, remediation, publication, retention, and domain interpretation caller-owned.
outOfScope: Do not infer private facts, account details, source contents, production readiness, or hidden approval state.
Do not decide publication, deployment, remediation ownership, tracker routing, retention, purchasing, or other real-world action.
Do not infer domain-specific systems, private repositories, or private programs from placeholder wording.
Do not treat generic evidence labels as proof outside the caller-owned packet.
